origin.name: Trinidad and Tobago
origin.key_document: Death certificate from Registrar General's Department (English language)
origin.source: FCDO Trinidad and Tobago travel advice, updated 2025; British High Commission Port of Spain guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Trinidad and Tobago is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option alongside full body repatriation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Death certificate from Registrar General's Department (English language)
special_rules: Trinidad and Tobago issues death certificates in English, which simplifies UK documentation requirements.
Police clearance is required for all unnatural or suspicious deaths.
Deaths on Tobago involve additional logistics for transfer to Trinidad before air repatriation.
An export permit from the relevant authority is required for all repatriations.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. Documentation is in English. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British High Commission in Port of Spain can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Trinidad and Tobago to UK Repatriation Guide
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Trinidad and Tobago. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-3 weeks. Registrar General, export permit, and air cargo all handled. Contact us 24/7.
